name: pypiprivate
vendor: Helpshift (created/maintained by Vineet Naik; repo under the helpshift GitHub org)
cost: Free (open source, MIT). You only pay for the storage (S3/Azure/disk) and whatever web server/CDN you put in front.
last_updated: 2025-06-20 (last commit on master, dependabot/SonarQube maintenance; latest PyPI release 0.5.0 on 2020-09-19)
perf_notes: No published benchmarks; it is a publish-time CLI, not a running server. Cost is dominated by the storage backend's upload latency (S3/Azure/disk). The simple index is fully regenerated on each publish (it lists every package/file), so publish time grows with repository size. Serving throughput/latency is entirely a property of whatever static host you choose (nginx / S3 static hosting / CDN), not of pypiprivate itself.
scale_notes: No server component, so there is nothing to cluster. Read/serving scale and availability are inherited from the static host or object store (e.g. S3 is effectively HA). The tool has no locking or coordination for concurrent publishers, so simultaneous publishes can race on the regenerated index. No HA/multi-node features in the tool itself.
summary: A small Python CLI that builds a PEP 503 static simple-index and uploads it plus your sdists/wheels to pluggable storage (local filesystem, AWS S3, or Azure Blob), which you then serve yourself behind a reverse proxy as a private package index.
extras: Architecture is a publish-time CLI (pypi-private publish <pkg> <version>) plus a pluggable storage abstraction, not a long-running server
Pluggable storage backends: local filesystem, AWS S3, and Azure Blob (azure-storage-blob extra)
Auth, TLS, and read access are deliberately delegated to an external reverse proxy (nginx) and/or the object store's IAM; the tool implements no live serving layer

Credentials are taken only from environment variables (PP_S3_ACCESS_KEY/PP_S3_SECRET_KEY/PP_S3_SESSION_TOKEN, PP_AZURE_CONN_STR) to avoid plaintext secrets in config files; non-secret settings live in ~/.pypi-private.cfg
Honors boto3's standard credential chain / AWS profiles and STS session tokens
Idempotent publish: is_dist_published() skips re-uploading a dist file that already exists (basic overwrite protection)
Recommended deployment splits roles: read-write storage creds for publishers, read-only creds for the web server, HTTP Basic auth at the proxy for consumers
Output is plain static files (PEP 503 tree), so it composes with any static host or CDN
